texto: I don't ever react to a situation. My preference is to reflect upon the circumstances & act as if I were a baby who is in possession of a new toy to play with.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Yogesh Datta D.: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I don't ever react to a situation. My preference is to reflect upon the circumstances & act as if…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, play, react pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de react e situation.

texto: In life also, everything seems to be going great, we take the people who love us, for granted, just like the overlooked trees providing pleasant shade to us during the journey. And then the destiny strikes. Everything falls apart. We want nothing but the sight of those same people whom we had ignored. No one is around then as we leave them far behind during our pursuit of success.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Yogesh Datta D.: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “In life also, everything seems to be going great, we take the people who love us, for granted, ju…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sucesso, destiny, vida pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de viagem e vida.

texto: You don’t want to be a rich businessman, a renowned artist, or whatsoever profession you choose to be into. What you really want is to be happy with what you do and to bring happiness in the lives of the people whom you love and who love you.
explicacao: Citação de Yogesh Datta D. sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “You don’t want to be a rich businessman, a renowned artist, or whatsoever profession you choose t…” articula felicidade, happiness, love em registro de felicidade, com relevância para contextos como love e profession.
